vix.note: VIX fell 12.5% to 19.44 as semis and tech rebounded on Intel double upgrade and broad risk-on rotation

macro: Intel surges 9.3% on double upgrade as national security thesis reshapes the chip sector; VIX drops 12.5% to 19.44 on broad risk-on. Semis led the recovery with SMH +6.7% and INTC +9.3% on June 11, as analysts reframed Intel's domestic fab buildout as strategically essential to U.S. defense. Broadcom flagged further gross margin compression, a persistent headwind for the AI hardware trade. Meta raised 2026 CapEx guidance to $125-145B, accelerating AI infrastructure spend even as investors question the monetization timeline and free cash flow impact.
action: Watch INTC for follow-through above $120 as the national security re-rating attracts institutional flows; monitor AVGO margin compression as the key risk for the semiconductor trade into earnings season.

long.h: Intel surges 9% on double upgrade; Cramer calls it more important than Nvidia
long.p: Intel received a double analyst upgrade on June 11 with CNBC's Cramer naming it the number one chip stock, citing U.S. government support for domestic fabs as a national security imperative. INTC closed +9.3% to $116.96, its best single-day gain in months.

long.h: Nvidia pitches Vera AI CPU to Chinese clients, targeting August availability
long.p: Nvidia has begun pitching its new Vera central processors for AI data centers to Chinese clients, with order books opening and August availability targeted. The move marks Nvidia's attempt to capture China revenue amid ongoing export restrictions on its highest-end GPUs.

long.h: Meta raises 2026 CapEx to $125-145B; FCF at risk if AI monetization lags
long.p: Meta raised its 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion, risking negative free cash flow if margins do not keep pace with spending. The bull case centers on AI monetization via new products and a strong ad business with 26% revenue growth expected in 2026.

long.h: Broadcom warns gross margins will keep falling after earnings whiplash
long.p: Broadcom told investors to expect continued gross profit margin compression following its recent earnings release, adding a persistent headwind on top of the AI chip revenue guidance miss that sparked last week's sector-wide selloff. The stock is up 3.5% as markets stabilize but the margin overhang remains.

long.h: SpaceX IPO: Oppenheimer launches with 40% upside target; Tesla merger bets rise
long.p: Oppenheimer initiated SpaceX coverage ahead of Friday's IPO, predicting over 40% upside and calling it the only pure-play in commercial space. Separately, prediction markets are pricing rising odds of a Tesla-SpaceX merger, which could reshape the TSLA long-term thesis significantly.

short.h: Nvidia hires DC lobbyist Bruce Andrews to lead government affairs
short.p: Nvidia hired veteran lobbyist Bruce Andrews to run its Washington government affairs operation, a clear signal the company is moving to proactively shape AI and chip export policy at the federal level.

short.h: Oracle FY2026 CapEx hits $55.7B, topping forecast; Nvidia and AMD are key GPU suppliers
short.p: Oracle's capital expenditures exceeded its own $50B forecast, with Nvidia and AMD supplying the GPUs powering its cloud build-out. This provides a strong demand signal for the AI infrastructure cycle heading into H2 2026.

short.h: Apple WWDC 2026: Enhanced Siri AI unveiled; Wall Street reaction is measured
short.p: Apple showcased major Siri upgrades at WWDC 2026 powered by Apple Intelligence. Analysts note the improvements are solid but fall short of the breakout AI moment investors had anticipated from the iPhone ecosystem.

short.h: Intel's national security role frames it as strategically essential to U.S. chip policy
short.p: A Motley Fool analysis argues Intel's domestic fab buildout positions it as a national security asset, attracting U.S. government support -- but warns investors must still independently track margins, fab utilization, and competitive execution.

short.h: Meta data center drives $50K teacher bonuses in Louisiana via sales tax surge
short.p: A massive Meta data center in Louisiana has generated a surge in sales tax receipts large enough to fund $50,000 bonuses for local teachers, illustrating the economic scale and local impact of the AI infrastructure build-out.

notes.INTC: INTC surged 9.3% on June 11 following a double analyst upgrade. The national security thesis -- U.S. policy support for domestic semiconductor fabs -- is reshaping its long-term investment case. Key risks remain: margins, fab utilization, and share recovery vs TSMC. Next earnings: 2026-07-23.
notes.AAPL: WWDC 2026 unveiled enhanced Siri AI capabilities powered by Apple Intelligence; market reaction is measured as upgrades are solid but not a breakout moment. Q2 beat: EPS $2.01 vs $1.95 est. Next earnings 2026-07-30, EPS est $1.86. Premium valuation reflects AI ecosystem optionality.
notes.META: 2026 CapEx raised to $125-145B; FCF pressure is the key near-term risk if AI monetization lags spend. Ad revenue rebounding with 26% growth expected in 2026. Strong earnings track record -- Q2 beat $7.31 vs $6.70 est. Next earnings 2026-07-29, EPS est $7.18.
notes.GOOGL: Dominant AI search and cloud platform; massive Q2 beat (EPS $5.11 vs $2.64 est). Faces antitrust and AI disruption risk but executing well. Trading off highs amid broad tech rotation. Next earnings 2026-07-22, EPS est $2.86.
